title: Copacabana (nightclub)

infobox.value: 625 W 51st St, New York, NY 10019 New York City United States

is a New York City [nightclub](/wiki/Nightclub "Nightclub") that has existed in several locations. In earlier locations, many entertainers, such as [Danny Thomas](/wiki/Danny_Thomas "Danny Thomas"), [Pat Cooper](/wiki/Pat_Cooper "Pat Cooper"), and the comedy team of [Martin and Lewis](/wiki/Martin_and_Lewis "Martin and Lewis"), made their New York debuts at the Copacabana. The [Barry Manilow](/wiki/Barry_Manilow "Barry Manilow") song "[Copacabana](/wiki/Copacabana_(song) "Copacabana (song)")" (1978) is named after, and set in, the club. The nightclub was used as a setting in the films *[Goodfellas](/wiki/Goodfellas "Goodfellas")*, *[Raging Bull](/wiki/Raging_Bull "Raging Bull")*, *[Tootsie](/wiki/Tootsie "Tootsie")*, *[The Purple Rose of Cairo](/wiki/The_Purple_Rose_of_Cairo "The Purple Rose of Cairo")*, *[Carlito's Way](/wiki/Carlito%27s_Way "Carlito's Way")*, *[Lonely Boy](/wiki/Lonely_Boy_(film) "Lonely Boy (film)")*, *[The French Connection](/wiki/The_French_Connection_(film) "The French Connection (film)")*, *[Martin and Lewis](/wiki/Martin_and_Lewis_(film) "Martin and Lewis (film)")*, *[Green Book](/wiki/Green_Book_(film) "Green Book (film)")*, *[Beyond the Sea](/wiki/Beyond_the_Sea_(2004_film) "Beyond the Sea (2004 film)")*, *[The Irishman](/wiki/The_Irishman "The Irishman")*, and *[One Night in Miami](/wiki/One_Night_in_Miami_(film) "One Night in Miami (film)")*. It was also used in several plays, including [Barry Manilow](/wiki/Barry_Manilow "Barry Manilow")'s *[Copacabana](/wiki/Copacabana_(musical) "Copacabana (musical)")*. Also, the musical film *[Copacabana](/wiki/Copacabana_(1947_film) "Copacabana (1947 film)")* (1947), starring [Groucho Marx](/wiki/Groucho_Marx "Groucho Marx") and [Carmen Miranda](/wiki/Carmen_Miranda "Carmen Miranda"), takes place in the Copacabana, as does [the made-for-television film](/wiki/Copacabana_(1985_film) "Copacabana (1985 film)") based on the Manilow song, in which Manilow himself starred.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Nightclub in New York City

Copacabana

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

The **Copacabana** is a New York City [nightclub](/wiki/Nightclub "Nightclub") that has existed in several locations. In earlier locations, many entertainers, such as [Danny Thomas](/wiki/Danny_Thomas "Danny Thomas"), [Pat Cooper](/wiki/Pat_Cooper "Pat Cooper"), and the comedy team of [Martin and Lewis](/wiki/Martin_and_Lewis "Martin and Lewis"), made their New York debuts at the Copacabana. The [Barry Manilow](/wiki/Barry_Manilow "Barry Manilow") song "[Copacabana](/wiki/Copacabana_(song) "Copacabana (song)")" (1978) is named after, and set in, the club. The nightclub was used as a setting in the films *[Goodfellas](/wiki/Goodfellas "Goodfellas")*, *[Raging Bull](/wiki/Raging_Bull "Raging Bull")*, *[Tootsie](/wiki/Tootsie "Tootsie")*, *[The Purple Rose of Cairo](/wiki/The_Purple_Rose_of_Cairo "The Purple Rose of Cairo")*, *[Carlito's Way](/wiki/Carlito%27s_Way "Carlito's Way")*, *[Lonely Boy](/wiki/Lonely_Boy_(film) "Lonely Boy (film)")*, *[The French Connection](/wiki/The_French_Connection_(film) "The French Connection (film)")*, *[Martin and Lewis](/wiki/Martin_and_Lewis_(film) "Martin and Lewis (film)")*, *[Green Book](/wiki/Green_Book_(film) "Green Book (film)")*, *[Beyond the Sea](/wiki/Beyond_the_Sea_(2004_film) "Beyond the Sea (2004 film)")*, *[The Irishman](/wiki/The_Irishman "The Irishman")*, and *[One Night in Miami](/wiki/One_Night_in_Miami_(film) "One Night in Miami (film)")*. It was also used in several plays, including [Barry Manilow](/wiki/Barry_Manilow "Barry Manilow")'s *[Copacabana](/wiki/Copacabana_(musical) "Copacabana (musical)")*. Also, the musical film *[Copacabana](/wiki/Copacabana_(1947_film) "Copacabana (1947 film)")* (1947), starring [Groucho Marx](/wiki/Groucho_Marx "Groucho Marx") and [Carmen Miranda](/wiki/Carmen_Miranda "Carmen Miranda"), takes place in the Copacabana, as does [the made-for-television film](/wiki/Copacabana_(1985_film) "Copacabana (1985 film)") based on the Manilow song, in which Manilow himself starred.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Copacabana_(nightclub)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### The 1940s to the 1960s

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Copacabana_(nightclub)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: The 1940s to the 1960s")]

[![A photograph of Willie Colón performing opening night at the new Copacabana.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/96/The_Copacabana.jpg/500px-The_Copacabana.jpg)](/wiki/File:The_Copacabana.jpg)

Willie Colón performing opening night at the new Copacabana[[1]](#cite_note-1) on July 12, 2011, in Times Square, New York City

The Copacabana (named after [Copacabana beach](/wiki/Copacabana,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Copacabana, Rio de Janeiro") in Rio de Janeiro) opened on November 10, 1940,[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) at 10 East 60th Street in New York City. Although the name of Monte Proser was on the lease, he had a powerful partner: mob boss [Frank Costello](/wiki/Frank_Costello "Frank Costello"). Proser (1904–1973), a native Englishman, was a well-connected nightclub owner and press agent whose various clients included [Walt Disney](/wiki/Walt_Disney "Walt Disney"), [Maria Montez](/wiki/Maria_Montez "Maria Montez"), [Mary Pickford](/wiki/Mary_Pickford "Mary Pickford"), and the [Ziegfeld Follies](/wiki/Ziegfeld_Follies "Ziegfeld Follies").[[4]](#cite_note-4) Costello put [Jules Podell](/wiki/Jules_Podell "Jules Podell") on the scene to look after his interests; Podell had a police record and would not have been an acceptable front man for the business, and indeed, the club faced tax problems and a [racketeering](/wiki/Racketeering "Racketeering") investigation in 1944. However, by 1948, such pressure had lessened; Proser was out, and Podell was the official owner.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

The Copacabana had Brazilian decor and Latin-themed orchestras, while the menu featured Chinese food.[[6]](#cite_note-drink-6) The club was also known for its chorus line, "The Copacabana Girls".[[7]](#cite_note-UNLV-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) As early as 1945 it also featured performances of "sweet" [big-band](/wiki/Big-band "Big-band") music by [Shep Fields](/wiki/Shep_Fields "Shep Fields") and his Rippling Rhythm Orchestra which were broadcast live on the [WOR Radio Network](/wiki/WOR_Radio_Network "WOR Radio Network").[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Podell originally had a strict "[no blacks](/wiki/Racial_segregation "Racial segregation")" policy. In 1944, [Harry Belafonte](/wiki/Harry_Belafonte "Harry Belafonte"), then a member of the [U.S. Navy](/wiki/United_States_Navy "United States Navy"), was denied entry with a date. Eventually, Podell was persuaded to change his policy and Belafonte returned in the 1950s as a headliner at the club. [Sammy Davis Jr.](/wiki/Sammy_Davis_Jr. "Sammy Davis Jr.") shattered attendance records with his run in May 1964 and [Sam Cooke](/wiki/Sam_Cooke "Sam Cooke") performed there on July 8, 1964, resulting in the LP *[Sam Cooke at the Copa](/wiki/Sam_Cooke_at_the_Copa "Sam Cooke at the Copa")*. In July 1965, [the Supremes](/wiki/The_Supremes "The Supremes") made their debut there, resulting in [Motown Records](/wiki/Motown_Records "Motown Records") booking [the Temptations](/wiki/The_Temptations "The Temptations"), [Martha and the Vandellas](/wiki/Martha_and_the_Vandellas "Martha and the Vandellas"), and [Marvin Gaye](/wiki/Marvin_Gaye "Marvin Gaye") to perform at the Copa over the next few years. [The Supremes](/wiki/The_Supremes "The Supremes") also recorded [The Supremes at the Copa](/wiki/The_Supremes_at_the_Copa "The Supremes at the Copa"), a live album, there in 1965 that just missed the Top 10, peaking at #11. [Marvin Gaye](/wiki/Marvin_Gaye "Marvin Gaye") also recorded a live album, as did The Temptations. The Supremes, who proved to be the most successful of all the Motown acts, released *The Supremes: Live at the Copa Expanded Edition* in 2012, featuring the much-sought-after original repertoire.[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)

[Dean Martin and Jerry Lewis](/wiki/Martin_and_Lewis "Martin and Lewis") were frequent performers at the club and performed their last show there on July 25, 1956, which can be seen in the TV movie *[Martin and Lewis](/wiki/Martin_and_Lewis_(film) "Martin and Lewis (film)")* (2002).

This nightclub achieved a degree of notoriety due to a May 16, 1957, incident involving members of the [New York Yankees](/wiki/New_York_Yankees "New York Yankees"). On that evening, teammates [Mickey Mantle](/wiki/Mickey_Mantle "Mickey Mantle"), [Whitey Ford](/wiki/Whitey_Ford "Whitey Ford"), [Hank Bauer](/wiki/Hank_Bauer "Hank Bauer"), [Yogi Berra](/wiki/Yogi_Berra "Yogi Berra"), [Johnny Kucks](/wiki/Johnny_Kucks "Johnny Kucks"), and [Billy Martin](/wiki/Billy_Martin "Billy Martin"), along with the wives of all but Martin, arrived at the nightclub to celebrate Martin's birthday. Sammy Davis Jr. happened to be the headliner. During the performance, a group of apparently [intoxicated](/wiki/Drunkenness "Drunkenness") bowlers started to interfere with Davis' act, even hurling [racial slurs](/wiki/List_of_ethnic_slurs "List of ethnic slurs") at him. This behavior incensed the Yankees, especially Martin, since his roommate was [Elston Howard](/wiki/Elston_Howard "Elston Howard"), the first black player to join the Yankees. Tensions erupted between the two groups, and the resulting fracas made newspaper headlines. Several of the Yankees were fined. One of the bowlers, a Bronx deli owner, ended up with a concussion and a broken jaw, and sued Bauer for [aggravated assault](/wiki/Aggravated_assault "Aggravated assault"); the case was thrown out for insufficient evidence. Martin was later traded from the Yankees to the [Kansas City Athletics](/wiki/Kansas_City_Athletics "Kansas City Athletics"), with this incident cited as a main cause.[[14]](#cite_note-BB1-14)

### The 1960s and the 1970s

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Copacabana_(nightclub)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: The 1960s and the 1970s")]

Until 1972, mafioso [Joe Gallo](/wiki/Joe_Gallo "Joe Gallo") operated the venue.[[15]](#cite_note-15) After Jules Podell died in 1973, it was closed for three years.[[16]](#cite_note-NYT-16) It reopened in 1976, at the height of the [disco](/wiki/Disco "Disco") era, and operated as a [discothèque](/wiki/Discoth%C3%A8que "Discothèque").[[17]](#cite_note-17) [Barry Manilow](/wiki/Barry_Manilow "Barry Manilow")'s hit 1978 song "[Copacabana (At The Copa)](/wiki/Copacabana_(At_The_Copa) "Copacabana (At The Copa)")" referred to the club; the song later became the basis for [the made-for-television film](/wiki/Copacabana_(1985_film) "Copacabana (1985 film)") in which Manilow himself starred; as of February 2021, it was not known how much of the film's shooting used it as an actual location.

### After the 1970s

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Copacabana_(nightclub)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: After the 1970s")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/93/Copacabana_Nightclub_New_York_City_3.jpg/250px-Copacabana_Nightclub_New_York_City_3.jpg)](/wiki/File:Copacabana_Nightclub_New_York_City_3.jpg)

The bar inside the Copacabana, 2011

In 1992, then-owner Peter Dorn moved the club from its original location to 617 West [57th Street](/wiki/57th_Street_(Manhattan) "57th Street (Manhattan)"). Dorn charged landlord Nicola Biase with "not liking Hispanics", the stated reason for the move.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

In 2001, the club was forced to move for a third time to 560 [West 34th Street](/wiki/34th_Street_(Manhattan) "34th Street (Manhattan)") and [Eleventh Avenue](/wiki/Eleventh_Avenue_(Manhattan) "Eleventh Avenue (Manhattan)") on the west side of [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan"), when its landlord terminated its lease early to build office towers on the site. Since then it has presented mostly [hip-hop](/wiki/Hip-hop "Hip-hop") and [salsa](/wiki/Salsa_music "Salsa music") acts.[[16]](#cite_note-NYT-16)

On January 20, 2007, the club announced that it would have to move by July 1 because its current location was condemned due to the construction of the extension of the [7 line](/wiki/7_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "7 (New York City Subway service)") of the [New York City Subway](/wiki/New_York_City_Subway "New York City Subway"), as well as the construction of the now-cancelled [World Product Center](/wiki/World_Product_Center "World Product Center").[[19]](#cite_note-19) June 30 of the same year was the last night the club was open, with [El Gran Combo](/wiki/El_Gran_Combo "El Gran Combo") performing there.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] From late 2007 until the club reopened in 2011, the club was sharing space with the Columbus 72 nightclub, which shares the same owners.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In April 2010, the club owners were approved for a [liquor license](/wiki/Liquor_license "Liquor license") to operate the club in a new location at 760–766 8th Avenue, on the second and third floors.[[20]](#cite_note-20) In November 2010, the club owners were granted permission to allow dancing by restaurant patrons as well as the general public, not limited to private parties and catered events.[[21]](#cite_note-21) On July 12, 2011, the club re-opened to the public in [Times Square](/wiki/Times_Square "Times Square") at 268 West [47th Street](/wiki/47th_Street_(Manhattan) "47th Street (Manhattan)"). The first performer at the new location was world-renowned [salsa musician](/wiki/Salsa_music "Salsa music") [Willie Colón](/wiki/Willie_Col%C3%B3n "Willie Colón").[[22]](#cite_note-22)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4b/Copacabana_Nightclub_New_York_City_4.jpg/250px-Copacabana_Nightclub_New_York_City_4.jpg)](/wiki/File:Copacabana_Nightclub_New_York_City_4.jpg)

Inside the club in 2011

On May 26, 2020, the club announced that it had closed due to the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic"), and that it planned to reopen in 2021 at another location.[[23]](#cite_note-Warerkar-23) It eventually reopened in February 2022 at 625 West 51st Street under the direction of Ruben Cabrera.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

## Outside New York City

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Copacabana_(nightclub)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Outside New York City")]

A second Copacabana—the first outside New York—was readied for a grand opening in [Fort Lauderdale, Florida](/wiki/Fort_Lauderdale,_Florida "Fort Lauderdale, Florida") on September 13, 2012, in the historic Las Olas District at 219 S. Andrews Avenue. On January 24, 2013, the location closed.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Copacabana_(nightclub)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Copa Room](/wiki/Copa_Room "Copa Room"), now-defunct [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas_Strip "Las Vegas Strip") nightclub at The [Sands Hotel](/wiki/Sands_Hotel "Sands Hotel")
* [Latin Quarter](/wiki/Latin_Quarter_(nightclub) "Latin Quarter (nightclub)"), competitor with a similar history
